articles.title: Narrowed to Clear the Art: The Enablement Cost of Wyeth’s Unit Dosage Claims
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇撰

在Wyeth LLC v. AstraZeneca Pharmaceuticals LP案中，案号2024-2325（联邦巡回法院，2026年7月9日），联邦巡回法院维持了即决判决，推翻了一项1.075亿美元的陪审团裁决，认定Wyeth主张的癌症治疗权利要求因缺乏35 U.S.C. § 112(a)规定的充分公开而无效。由Lourie法官主笔的裁决得到包括Linn法官和Hughes法官在内的陪审团一致同意。涉案专利（美国专利第10,603,314号和第10,596,162号）主张一种治疗吉非替尼和厄洛替尼耐药的非小细胞肺癌（NSCLC）的方法，即每日施用一种与特定半胱氨酸残基共价结合的不可逆EGFR抑制剂的"单位剂量"。特拉华州陪审团认定权利要求有效，并认定AstraZeneca的Tagrisso（奥希替尼）构成诱导侵权。Matthew Kennelly法官（通过指定出庭）批准了即决判决，认定权利要求无效，联邦巡回法院同意该裁定。关键问题在于：说明书未能教化本领域技术人员如何在不过度实验的情况下确定所主张化合物的治疗有效每日剂量。我在SCOTUSGate发布了庭审笔录和录音。

Wyeth在审查过程中添加到权利要求语言中的路径有助于理解这一结果。Wyeth在审查过程中增加了"每日向患者施用""单位剂量"的要求，说明书将单位剂量定义为"旨在产生预期治疗效果的量"。在庭审中双方均认可该语言是为克服现有技术而添加的。这种限缩成功避免了现有技术的障碍。但这也改变了专利必须充分公开的内容。基于说明书的体外数据，一项关于杀死癌细胞的权利要求可以得到支持。但关于每日向活体患者施用有效剂量的权利要求，则要求说明书教化如何确定该剂量，而这正是说明书失败之处。专利实务就是不断穿针引线。在此，拯救权利要求免于现有技术的限缩，正是导致权利要求缺乏充分公开的同一限缩。该结论可能是正确的。我关注的是合议庭采取的路径，该路径依赖的充分公开语言比其引用的案例实际确立的范围更为宽泛。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

In Wyeth LLC v. AstraZeneca Pharmaceuticals LP, No. 2024-2325 (Fed. Cir. July 9, 2026), the Federal Circuit affirmed a judgment as a matter of law that wiped out a $107.5 million jury verdict, holding Wyeth's asserted cancer-treatment claims invalid for lack of enablement under 35 U.S.C. § 112(a). Judge Lourie wrote for a unanimous panel that also included Judges Linn and Hughes. The patents (U.S. Patents 10,603,314 and 10,596,162) claim a method of treating gefitinib and erlotinib resistant non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) by administering a daily "unit dosage" of an irreversible EGFR inhibitor that covalently binds a specific cysteine residue. A Delaware jury found the claims not invalid and found that AstraZeneca's Tagrisso (osimertinib) induced infringement. Judge Matthew Kennelly, sitting by designation, granted JMOL of invalidity, and the Federal Circuit agreed. The key problem here: the specification does not teach a skilled artisan how to arrive at a therapeutically effective daily dose for the claimed compounds without undue experimentation. I posted the transcript and audio of the argument at SCOTUSGate.

The path Wyeth took to its claim language helps understand the results. During prosecution, Wyeth added the requirement that a "unit dosage" be "administer[ed] daily to the patient," and the specification defines that unit dosage as an amount "calculated to produce the desired therapeutic effect." At argument both sides agreed that the language was added to get past prior art. The narrowing worked to avoid the art. But, it also changed what the patent had to enable. A claim to killing cancer cells was enabled based upon the spec's in vitro data. But, the claim to administering an effective dose to a living patient, every day, requires the specification to teach how to reach that dose, and that is where the spec failed. Patent practice is about constantly threading the needle. Here, the narrowing that saved the claims from the prior art is the same narrowing that left them non-enabled. That conclusion is likely right. My concern is the route the panel takes to it, which leans on enablement language that reads more broadly than the cases it cites actually hold.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.

articles.title: Words are Magic: What Counts as Applicant Admitted Prior Art in In re GE Vernova Hitachi
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇（Dennis Crouch）撰写  
“言语具有魔力，法官阁下……词典就是我们的咒语书。”这是专利律师瑞安·阿利（Ryan Alley）在上周口头辩论中的回答。当时坎宁安法官（Judge Cunningham）询问，是否需要在申请人背景陈述被视为自认现有技术之前，必须使用“某种意义上的咒语般的措辞”。坎宁安法官追问（“您是不是像哈利·波特那样？我需要更多依据。”），但阿利坚持主张：放弃权利必须使用众人皆知的明确用语。这场交锋抓住了待决案件——GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy Americas LLC案（联邦巡回上诉法院案号25-1410，2026年7月7日辩论）——的核心问题：申请人的说明书陈述何时会成为美国专利商标局（USPTO）可用来反驳权利要求的“申请人自认的现有技术”（AAPA）？

GE Vernova Hitachi的美国专利申请号16/237,637要求保护在地表建造反应堆井筒并将其沉入地下的核设施建造方法。审查员以该申请自身两段关于竖井下沉机（VSM）的背景描述作为AAPA，结合对比文件（ Mancini，美国专利公开号2014/0050531）驳回了权利要求11。专利审判和上诉委员会（PTAB）维持了驳回决定，将背景段落和附图1视为对所述方法已属公知的技术承认。首席法官摩尔（Chief Judge Moore）、坎宁安法官以及指定审理的纽约南区法院阿伦·苏布拉马尼安法官（Judge Arun Subramanian）审理了此上诉，美国专利商标局局长一方由莫琳·奎勒（Maureen Queler）出庭陈述。完整辩论记录和音频可查阅SCOTUSGate网站。

更新：联邦巡回法院并未等待太久。2026年7月9日即辩论后两天上午，法院以第36条判决（Rule 36 judgment）作出无意见的维持裁决，直接肯定了委员会决定。该全体法官判决未载明推理理由且无先例约束力，因此无法判断合议庭是基于程序弃权还是自认问题的实体裁决作出决定。无论如何，权利要求11的驳回决定仍然有效，而“咒语般措辞”的问题仍需等待后续案件澄清。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

"Words are magic, Your Honor. . . . Dictionaries are our spellbooks." That was patent attorney Ryan Alley’s answer at oral argument last week when Judge Cunningham asked whether his client was demanding "essentially sort of magic words" before an applicant’s background statements can be treated as admitted prior art. Cunningham pressed back ("Are you like Harry Potter? I’m going to need a little bit more here."), but Alley held firm: waiving a right requires words everyone understands as giving up that right. The exchange captures the core question in the pending case of In re GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy Americas LLC, No. 25-1410 (Fed. Cir. argued July 7, 2026): when do an applicant’s own specification statements become applicant admitted prior art (AAPA) that the USPTO can turn against the claims?

GE Vernova Hitachi’s U.S. Patent Application No. 16/237,637 claims methods of building nuclear facilities by constructing a reactor shaft at the surface and sinking it into the earth. The examiner rejected claim 11 as obvious, using the application’s own two-paragraph background description of vertical shaft sinking machines (VSMs) as AAPA in combination with a reference (Mancini, U.S. Patent Pub. No. 2014/0050531). The PTAB affirmed, treating the background paragraphs and Figure 1 as an admission that the described approach was already known. Chief Judge Moore, Judge Cunningham, and Judge Arun Subramanian (S.D.N.Y., sitting by designation) heard the appeal, with Maureen Queler arguing for the USPTO Director. The full transcript and audio are at SCOTUSGate.

UPDATE: The Federal Circuit did not wait long. On the morning of July 9, 2026, two days after argument, the court issued a Rule 36 judgment summarily affirming the Board without opinion. The per curiam judgment carries no reasoning and no precedential weight, so we cannot know whether the panel acted on forfeiture or on the merits of the admission question. Either way, the rejection of claim 11 stands, and the magic words question waits for another case.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
